methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $2.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ICEYE is a Series E-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $650M+ and currently holds a post-money valuation of $2.8B. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Astranis at $3.5B, Apex Space at $1.0B, and Stoke Space at $1.0B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the $2.8B last post-money valuation, implying $3.9B. Peer-median anchoring across the 3 comparable private companies yields $1.0B, though the range spans $1.0B to $3.5B. Public-comp P/S anchoring is not applicable without disclosed revenue figures. The public Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 64.2x P/S across six tickers, but cannot be applied to ICEYE without revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M to $4.5B, with a median of $2.5B. The wide spread reflects the divergence between peer-median ($1.0B) and stage-progression ($3.9B) methods. Key uncertainties include satellite constellation revenue visibility, the comparability of ICEYE's synthetic-aperture radar business model relative to optical and communications-focused peers, and execution risk on near-term commercial contracts.
